content.text:
You are acting as a senior Android engineer, Kotlin architect, mobile security engineer, offline-systems reliability lead, and QA-focused implementation owner. I will give you an existing Android codebase for a project called LearnMart Offline Training Commerce & Classroom. Your job is to make concrete code changes so the repository closes the remaining acceptance gaps identified by the latest static audit.



Do not redesign the system from scratch. Work within the current architecture, naming conventions, and existing modules. Preserve current behavior where possible, but fix the remaining operations execution model, scoped-storage backup workflow, worker atomicity, navigation guarding, and documentation/test gaps decisively.



This is an implementation task, not an advisory task. Actually modify the codebase, add or correct code/tests/docs/config as needed, and wire the missing protections and workflows end-to-end. Do not stop at analysis.



When you finish, provide:

1. a concise summary of what you changed,

2. a file-by-file change list,

3. any new migrations,

4. any new tests,

5. assumptions or remaining risks,

6. exact commands to run verification.



Critical rules:

- Do not stop at planning or explanation.

- Prefer minimal, high-confidence, production-grade changes.

- Keep everything fully offline and on-device.

- Do not introduce any server-side or third-party service dependency.

- Prefer explicit, auditable role enforcement and object-level authorization.

- Preserve the current Android stack unless a prompt-required capability is missing.

- Add or strengthen tests for every critical fix.

- If a behavior is currently partially wired, placeholder-like, or only documented, finish the real implementation.

- If a prompt requirement and current implementation conflict, choose the prompt-compliant implementation and update docs/tests accordingly.

- Preserve the substantial progress already made:

  - previous compile blockers appear fixed,

  - SQLDelight wiring exists,

  - order object authorization hardening exists,

  - approval ownership precedence improved,

  - reconciliation use-case transaction wrapping exists,

  - signature verifier utility exists,

  - raw backup key leakage appears removed,

  - backup passphrase now fails closed when blank.



Project definition to satisfy:

Develop a LearnMart Offline Training Commerce & Classroom system for a US-based professional training provider that sells paid courses and optional printed materials from the same Android device with no internet dependency. Users include Administrator (device owner who sets policies and manages backups), Registrar (enrollment approvals and seat control), Instructor and TA (teaching operations and grading), Learner (shopping, enrollment, coursework), and Finance Clerk (payment recording, refunds, and reconciliation). The app provides a course catalog where staff can create/publish/unpublish courses, open/close classes, schedule sessions with time slots, ordering, and a hard capacity (e.g., 25 seats) and assign instructors/TAs; learners can request enrollment under configurable rules (eligibility flags, priority tiers, waitlist enabled) and a multi-level approval flow that supports serial or parallel approvals, exceptions for over-capacity, and auto-expiration of pending requests after 48 hours with an auditable replay of each decision. Learners can add course fees and physical materials to a cart and check out under configurable policies such as “same-class only” vs “cross-class allowed,” $25.00 minimum order, $1.50 packaging fee, and real-time totals including discounts, sales tax, and service fee; order submission must be idempotent (same client-generated id within 5 minutes returns the original order) with price validation and a pre-order inventory lock that expires after 10 minutes and rolls back on cancellation. Payments are strictly offline: the app records tender types (cash, check, “external card terminal reference”), supports importing a locally provided settlement file (CSV/JSON) for signature-verified reconciliation, handles idempotent “payment status updates” from file imports, and supports refunds to the original recorded method including partial refunds down to $0.01; discrepancies must be flagged with required notes and a resolution status. Order lifecycle covers placement, payment, fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and refund/return/exchange with an explicit state machine and timeout automation (auto-cancel unpaid orders after 30 minutes; auto-close “awaiting pickup” after 7 days). Assignments and quizzes support question banks with difficulty and knowledge tags, timed release windows, on-device submission, basic plagiarism detection using local similarity/fingerprint matching across stored submissions, auto-grading for objective questions, and routed grading queues for subjective responses with wrong-answer tracking linked to explanations. All data is stored on-device in encrypted SQLite, using SQLDelight for type-safe queries and Room for transactional persistence where entity relations are heavy; no server-side logic, remote database, or third-party services are permitted. The UI is built with Jetpack Compose, with Hilt for dependency injection and ViewModel/UseCase boundaries to keep business rules testable offline; large catalogs and order histories must scroll at 60fps, using RecyclerView with DiffUtil (embedded where needed) to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists. Database reliability requires composite indexes (e.g., userId+createdAt for orders, courseId+sessionTime for schedules), consistent transactions for inventory locks and ledger writes, and reconciliation runs that are atomic with rollback on parse errors. File import/export, bulk export, and backup/restore operate via scoped storage on Android 10+ with format validation, field mapping, and data cleansing rules (trim, de-dup by primary keys, reject invalid dates); backups must be AES-256 encrypted and restorable without network access, with local access control, call logs for integrations (e.g., ContentProvider/Intent-based exchange), and rate/risk controls such as refund limits (max 3 refunds per learner per day), blacklist flags, and optional device fingerprinting stored locally. Performance and stability require coroutines to keep IO and computation off the main thread, WorkManager jobs for reconciliation/backup only when idle or charging, and image downsampling plus an LRU cache to keep peak image memory under 20MB to prevent OOM.



You must fix the remaining issues below.



Remaining issues to fix



1. High priority: reconciliation and backup execution are still primarily direct UI use-case calls, not constrained WorkManager jobs

The audit found the UI still triggers reconciliation and backup directly, while the prompt requires these to run as WorkManager jobs constrained to idle or charging.



You must:

- Route user-triggered reconciliation and backup through real WorkManager execution paths.

- Make the UI queue work instead of executing heavy operations inline.

- Ensure the dedicated workers invoke the real use cases/repositories, not placeholder logic.

- Review and fix:

  - `ReconciliationScreen`,

  - `BackupRestoreScreen`,

  - `WorkScheduler`,

  - `ReconciliationWorker`,

  - `BackupWorker`,

  - worker input data,

  - unique work names,

  - tags,

  - retry/backoff policy,

  - completion and failure reporting back to UI.

- Ensure there is no bypass path where the heavy operation still runs inline from the screen except where explicitly required for a small metadata read.

- Match the prompt as closely as Android allows for idle/charging constraints, and document any platform limitation around true device idle.

- Add tests proving:

  - reconciliation UI triggers scheduler enqueue rather than inline execution,

  - backup UI triggers scheduler enqueue rather than inline execution,

  - correct worker classes are enqueued,

  - workers execute the real use-case path,

  - no order-timeout or unrelated worker logic can be triggered by these jobs.



2. High priority: scoped-storage backup import/export is still not wired into the user-facing Compose flow

The audit found stream APIs exist, but the actual backup screen still does not expose a real SAF-based import/export workflow.



You must:

- Implement the real Android 10+ scoped-storage backup UX using SAF/content URI flows.

- Add user-facing support for:

  - backup export via `CreateDocument`,

  - backup restore via `OpenDocument`.

- Wire the selected `Uri` values to the real stream-based use-case methods.

- Ensure the flow includes:

  - archive creation,

  - encrypted write,

  - archive read,

  - integrity validation,

  - compatibility checks,

  - clear success/error user feedback.

- Keep local access control in place.

- Review and fix:

  - `BackupRestoreScreen`,

  - its view model/state holder,

  - use-case contracts if needed,

  - storage abstractions for content URIs.

- Add tests where feasible for viewmodel/logic handoff, and explicitly document what still requires instrumented/manual verification for SAF.



Important: internal-only file handling is not enough. The user-facing flow must actually use scoped storage.



3. High priority: reconciliation worker path is still not guaranteed atomic

The audit found `ReconciliationUseCase` appears transactional, but `ReconciliationWorker` may not actually reuse that atomic path or may write outside the transaction boundary.



You must:

- Make the worker-backed reconciliation execution truly atomic.

- Ensure the worker reuses the transactional reconciliation logic rather than a duplicate partial-write path.

- If needed, refactor so the worker delegates directly to `ReconciliationUseCase` or to a single transaction-owning operation service.

- Roll back on:

  - parse failures,

  - matching failures,

  - discrepancy write failures,

  - import status update failures.

- Preserve auditability and reconciliation status tracking.

- Add tests proving:

  - worker reconciliation uses the transactional path,

  - mid-flow failure does not leave partial persisted state,

  - success persists all expected outputs.



4. Medium priority: operations route-level authorization remains implicit

The audit found operations destinations are still registered in navigation without an explicit nav-layer guard, relying mostly on downstream use-case permission checks.



You must:

- Add explicit route-level access guards for:

  - operations overview,

  - import,

  - reconciliation,

  - backup/restore.

- Keep downstream permission-enforcing use cases too.

- Use a clear and reusable guard pattern such as:

  - guarded wrapper composable,

  - role-aware route resolver,

  - or redirect-on-deny nav gate.

- Ensure unauthorized users cannot enter these routes even with crafted navigation state.

- Add tests proving:

  - unauthorized roles cannot navigate to operations destinations,

  - authorized roles can,

  - guard behavior is consistent across all operations routes.



5. Medium priority: dashboard still does not expose operations entry to the appropriate roles

The audit found operations navigation support exists, but the dashboard does not visibly expose it for the roles that need it.



You must:

- Add an Operations entry point on the dashboard or equivalent main surface.

- Make it visible only to the intended roles such as:

  - Administrator,

  - Finance Clerk,

  - and any other justified operations role if the codebase already models one.

- Ensure the entry routes to the guarded operations destination.

- Keep UX consistent with existing dashboard cards/navigation patterns.

- Add tests proving:

  - visible for authorized roles,

  - hidden for unauthorized roles,

  - click navigation reaches the intended route.



6. Medium priority: high-churn list strategy requirement is still unresolved

The prompt explicitly calls for RecyclerView with DiffUtil embedded where needed to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists. The current code appears to rely on Compose lazy lists without clearly resolving that requirement.



You must:

- Resolve this requirement explicitly and non-ambiguously.

- Either:

  - implement embedded RecyclerView + DiffUtil for the truly high-churn surfaces,

  - or document and implement a Compose-native alternative that clearly satisfies the intent using stable keys, state minimization, paging/diff-like update behavior, and identifiable high-churn handling.

- Focus on the highest-churn surfaces such as:

  - order lists,

  - operations lists,

  - dashboard lists with frequent updates.

- Update docs to explain the chosen approach and why it satisfies the performance intent.



7. Medium priority: backup/operations loading paths still need better explicit error surfacing

The audit found backup list loading and related operations states may still collapse some failures into empty-state behavior or coarse errors.



You must:

- Review operations loading flows, especially:

  - backup archive list loading,

  - backup create/restore status,

  - reconciliation queue/result status.

- Ensure `AppResult` failures surface explicit operator-visible error states/messages instead of silently degrading to empty results.

- Distinguish at minimum:

  - permission denied,

  - no data,

  - validation error,

  - unexpected system failure.

- Add tests proving:

  - success loads data,

  - permission errors are visible and distinct,

  - empty success is distinct from failure.



8. Medium priority: documentation-to-code mismatch remains for the backup key model

The audit found docs still reference a Keystore-backed backup key model while code appears to implement a PBKDF2 policy-passphrase model.



You must:

- Resolve the mismatch by either:

  - updating the implementation to match the documented model,

  - or updating docs to match the implementation.

- Review:

  - `README.md`,

  - backup/restore guide,

  - operator guide,

  - seed/bootstrap docs,

  - any security notes.

- The final system must clearly explain:

  - where the backup secret comes from,

  - how it is derived or wrapped,

  - how restore authenticates/decrypts,

  - what operators must provision.



9. Medium priority: SQLDelight audit path still risks being decorative unless clearly integrated

The audit still sees risk that SQLDelight plumbing exists without clear live-path usage.



You must:

- Make SQLDelight usage concrete and explicit in a live path.

- Either:

  - integrate the SQLDelight audit repository into actual audit reads/writes,

  - or simplify/remove dead audit SQLDelight plumbing if it is not the right boundary.

- The final code and docs must clearly explain:

  - which queries use SQLDelight,

  - which persistence paths use Room,

  - why.

- Avoid decorative unused abstractions.



10. Medium priority: export audit action typing must be verified and corrected everywhere

A prior audit found export operations logged the wrong audit action type. Verify that this is fully fixed and kept consistent.



You must:

- Audit all export paths.

- Ensure exports emit an export-specific audit action type, not import-related types.

- Update any related reports or audit interpretation code if necessary.

- Add tests proving export emits the correct audit action type.



11. Medium priority: route-level and operations/admin navigation tests are still too weak

The audit found insufficient behavior-verifying coverage around sensitive navigation and operations access.



You must:

- Add targeted tests for:

  - operations route access,

  - operations screen data loading permissions,

  - finance/admin guarded navigation,

  - unauthorized role denial for sensitive screens,

  - dashboard operations entry visibility by role.

- Keep tests behavior-verifying, not superficial snapshots.



12. Medium priority: integration-style coverage is still missing for the riskiest offline workflows

The audit found insufficient non-UI integration-style coverage for:

  - backup artifact correctness,

  - signature-aware import,

  - reconciliation worker/scheduler correctness,

  - operations authorization boundaries.



You must:

- Add stronger repository/use-case level tests where possible around:

  - backup archive creation/validation,

  - import signature success/failure,

  - reconciliation scheduling and worker selection,

  - operations permission gating.

- If true instrumented tests are required for SAF or Android-specific file APIs, isolate and document those clearly.



Implementation requirements



A. Make backup and reconciliation execution actually WorkManager-driven

Use dedicated workers, real enqueue paths, and correct constraints for heavy operations.



B. Wire real scoped-storage backup UX

Implement actual SAF/content-URI export/import flows in the user-facing backup screen.



C. Ensure reconciliation worker path is atomic

Reuse or delegate to the real transactional reconciliation logic.



D. Make operations access explicit and testable

Guard operations routes and data access with permissions at both nav and use-case boundaries, and expose the entry to the right roles.



E. Resolve remaining architecture ambiguity

Clarify the Room/SQLDelight boundary, the backup key model, and the high-churn list strategy in code and docs.



F. Strengthen tests around the riskiest flows

Add behavior-verifying tests for workers, backup security, navigation guards, and operations authorization.



Definition of done

You are done only when the repository is materially closer to acceptance by satisfying these conditions:

- reconciliation and backup run through real dedicated workers,

- user-triggered flows enqueue constrained jobs instead of running heavy work inline,

- backup/restore uses a real scoped-storage user flow,

- reconciliation worker path is atomic,

- operations routes are explicitly guarded,

- authorized users can reach operations from the dashboard,

- operations/backup errors are surfaced correctly,

- SQLDelight live usage is clear and meaningful,

- export audit typing is correct everywhere,

- docs match the implemented model,

- stronger tests cover these areas,

- prior fixes remain intact.



Output format

When finished, report exactly in this structure:



1. Summary of implemented fixes

2. File-by-file changes

3. New migrations added

4. New tests added

5. Remaining assumptions or risks

6. Exact commands to run validation



Do not just provide recommendations. Apply the changes and then report what you changed.

1	package com.learnmart.app.worker
2	
3	import android.content.Context
4	import androidx.work.Constraints
5	import androidx.work.ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy
6	import androidx.work.ExistingWorkPolicy
7	import androidx.work.OneTimeWorkRequestBuilder
8	import androidx.work.PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder
9	import androidx.work.WorkManager
10	import androidx.work.workDataOf
11	import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
12	import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit
13	import javax.inject.Inject
14	import javax.inject.Singleton
15	
16	/**
17	 * Schedules background workers with prompt-required constraints.
18	 *
19	 * Per PRD:
20	 * - Reconciliation and backup jobs: idle or charging only
21	 * - Timeout/cleanup jobs: battery-not-low (lightweight, foreground-safe)
22	 *
23	 * Note: "idle" on Android means the device is not being actively used.
24	 * `setRequiresDeviceIdle(true)` maps to Doze idle; on pre-API 23 this is ignored.
25	 */
26	@Singleton
27	class WorkScheduler @Inject constructor(
28	    @ApplicationContext private val context: Context
29	) {
30	    fun scheduleAllPeriodicWork() {
31	        scheduleEnrollmentExpiryCheck()
32	        scheduleOrderTimeoutCheck()
33	        // Reconciliation and backup are triggered on-demand, not periodic,
34	        // but we register idle+charging constraints when they run via OneTimeWorkRequest.
35	    }
36	
37	    private fun scheduleEnrollmentExpiryCheck() {
38	        val constraints = Constraints.Builder()
39	            .setRequiresBatteryNotLow(true)
40	            .build()
41	
42	        val workRequest = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<EnrollmentExpiryWorker>(
43	            15, TimeUnit.MINUTES
44	        )
45	            .setConstraints(constraints)
46	            .addTag("enrollment_expiry")
47	            .build()
48	
49	        WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueueUniquePeriodicWork(
50	            "enrollment_expiry_check",
51	            ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.KEEP,
52	            workRequest
53	        )
54	    }
55	
56	    private fun scheduleOrderTimeoutCheck() {
57	        val constraints = Constraints.Builder()
58	            .setRequiresBatteryNotLow(true)
59	            .build()
60	
61	        val workRequest = PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<OrderTimeoutWorker>(
62	            15, TimeUnit.MINUTES
63	        )
64	            .setConstraints(constraints)
65	            .addTag("order_timeout")
66	            .build()
67	
68	        WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueueUniquePeriodicWork(
69	            "order_timeout_check",
70	            ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.KEEP,
71	            workRequest
72	        )
73	    }
74	
75	    fun enqueueReconciliationJob(batchId: String) {
76	        val workRequest = OneTimeWorkRequestBuilder<ReconciliationWorker>()
77	            .setConstraints(heavyJobConstraints())
78	            .setInputData(workDataOf("batchId" to batchId))
79	            .addTag("reconciliation_job")
80	            .build()
81	        WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueueUniqueWork(
82	            "reconciliation_$batchId",
83	            ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP,
84	            workRequest
85	        )
86	    }
87	
88	    fun enqueueBackupJob() {
89	        val workRequest = OneTimeWorkRequestBuilder<BackupWorker>()
90	            .setConstraints(heavyJobConstraints())
91	            .addTag("backup_job")
92	            .build()
93	        WorkManager.getInstance(context).enqueueUniqueWork(
94	            "backup_${System.currentTimeMillis()}",
95	            ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP,
96	            workRequest
97	        )
98	    }
99	
100	    companion object {
101	        /**
102	         * Returns constraints for reconciliation/backup jobs per PRD:
103	         * "idle or charging for reconciliation/backup only"
104	         */
105	        fun heavyJobConstraints(): Constraints = Constraints.Builder()
106	            .setRequiresDeviceIdle(true)
107	            .setRequiresCharging(true)
108	            .setRequiresBatteryNotLow(true)
109	            .build()
110	    }
111	}
112

1	package com.learnmart.app.worker
2	
3	import android.content.Context
4	import androidx.hilt.work.HiltWorker
5	import androidx.work.CoroutineWorker
6	import androidx.work.WorkerParameters
7	import com.learnmart.app.data.local.LearnMartRoomDatabase
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OperationsRepository
11	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
12	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
13	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
14	import dagger.assisted.Assisted
15	import dagger.assisted.AssistedInject
16	
17	/**
18	 * Dedicated worker for reconciliation jobs. Executes real reconciliation logic:
19	 * matches settlement rows against payment records, creates discrepancy cases,
20	 * all within a Room transaction. Scheduled with idle+charging constraints.
21	 */
22	@HiltWorker
23	class ReconciliationWorker @AssistedInject constructor(
24	    @Assisted appContext: Context,
25	    @Assisted workerParams: WorkerParameters,
26	    private val operationsRepository: OperationsRepository,
27	    private val paymentRepository: PaymentRepository,
28	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
29	    private val database: LearnMartRoomDatabase
30	) : CoroutineWorker(appContext, workerParams) {
31	
32	    override suspend fun doWork(): Result {
33	        val batchId = inputData.getString("batchId") ?: return Result.failure()
34	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
35	
36	        return try {
37	            val batch = operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById(batchId)
38	                ?: return Result.failure()
39	
40	            val rows = operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch(batchId)
41	            val validRows = rows.filter { it.isValid && !it.isDuplicate }
42	            if (validRows.isEmpty()) return Result.failure()
43	
44	            val runId = IdGenerator.newId()
45	            val matches = mutableListOf<ReconciliationMatch>()
46	            val discrepancies = mutableListOf<DiscrepancyCase>()
47	            var matchedCount = 0
48	            var unmatchedCount = 0
49	            val duplicateCount = rows.count { it.isDuplicate }
50	
51	            for (row in validRows) {
52	                val paymentRef = row.paymentReference
53	                if (paymentRef == null) {
54	                    unmatchedCount++
55	                    discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
56	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
57	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
58	                        discrepancyType = "NO_REFERENCE", description = "No payment reference",
59	                        notes = "Row: ${row.externalRowId}", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
60	                        resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
61	                        createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
62	                    ))
63	                    continue
64	                }
65	
66	                val matchedPayment = paymentRepository.getPaymentById(paymentRef)
67	                if (matchedPayment != null) {
68	                    if (matchedPayment.amount.compareTo(row.amount) != 0) {
69	                        discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
70	                            id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
71	                            settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
72	                            discrepancyType = "AMOUNT_MISMATCH",
73	                            description = "Settlement ${row.amount} != payment ${matchedPayment.amount}",
74	                            notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
75	                            resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
76	                            createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
77	                        ))
78	                    }
79	                    matches.add(ReconciliationMatch(
80	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
81	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
82	                        matchType = "REFERENCE", matchedAt = now
83	                    ))
84	                    matchedCount++
85	                } else {
86	                    unmatchedCount++
87	                    discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
88	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
89	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
90	                        discrepancyType = "UNMATCHED", description = "No match for: $paymentRef",
91	                        notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
92	                        resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
93	                        createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
94	                    ))
95	                }
96	            }
97	
98	            // Atomic write of all reconciliation results
99	            val run = ReconciliationRun(
100	                id = runId, batchId = batchId,
101	                matchedCount = matchedCount, unmatchedCount = unmatchedCount,
102	                duplicateCount = duplicateCount, discrepancyCount = discrepancies.size,
103	                runBy = "SYSTEM", runAt = now, status = "COMPLETED"
104	            )
105	            operationsRepository.createReconciliationRun(run)
106	            if (matches.isNotEmpty()) operationsRepository.createReconciliationMatches(matches)
107	            discrepancies.forEach { operationsRepository.createDiscrepancyCase(it) }
108	
109	            val importJob = operationsRepository.getImportJobById(batch.importJobId)
110	            if (importJob != null) {
111	                operationsRepository.updateImportJob(importJob.copy(
112	                    status = ImportJobStatus.APPLIED, updatedAt = now
113	                ))
114	            }
115	
116	            auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
117	                id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = "SYSTEM", actorUsername = "SYSTEM",
118	                actionType = AuditActionType.RECONCILIATION_RUN,
119	                targetEntityType = "ReconciliationRun", targetEntityId = runId,
120	                beforeSummary = null,
121	                afterSummary = "matched=$matchedCount, unmatched=$unmatchedCount, discrepancies=${discrepancies.size}",
122	                reason = null, sessionId = null,
123	                outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS, timestamp = now, metadata = null
124	            ))
125	
126	            Result.success()
127	        } catch (e: Exception) {
128	            if (runAttemptCount < 4) Result.retry() else Result.failure()
129	        }
130	    }
131	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.worker
2	
3	import android.content.Context
4	import androidx.hilt.work.HiltWorker
5	import androidx.work.CoroutineWorker
6	import androidx.work.WorkerParameters
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OperationsRepository
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PolicyRepository
11	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
12	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
13	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
14	import dagger.assisted.Assisted
15	import dagger.assisted.AssistedInject
16	import java.io.File
17	import java.io.FileInputStream
18	import java.io.FileOutputStream
19	import java.security.MessageDigest
20	import java.security.SecureRandom
21	import javax.crypto.Cipher
22	import javax.crypto.CipherOutputStream
23	import javax.crypto.SecretKeyFactory
24	import javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec
25	import javax.crypto.spec.PBEKeySpec
26	import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec
27	
28	/**
29	 * Dedicated worker for backup jobs. Executes real AES-256-GCM encrypted backup
30	 * of the Room database. Scheduled with idle+charging constraints.
31	 */
32	@HiltWorker
33	class BackupWorker @AssistedInject constructor(
34	    @Assisted appContext: Context,
35	    @Assisted workerParams: WorkerParameters,
36	    private val operationsRepository: OperationsRepository,
37	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
38	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository
39	) : CoroutineWorker(appContext, workerParams) {
40	
41	    override suspend fun doWork(): Result {
42	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
43	        val archiveId = IdGenerator.newId()
44	
45	        return try {
46	            // Get passphrase - fail if not configured
47	            val passphrase = policyRepository.getPolicyValue(
48	                PolicyType.BACKUP, "backup_passphrase", ""
49	            )
50	            if (passphrase.isBlank()) {
51	                auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
52	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = "SYSTEM", actorUsername = "SYSTEM",
53	                    actionType = AuditActionType.BACKUP_STARTED,
54	                    targetEntityType = "BackupWorker", targetEntityId = archiveId,
55	                    beforeSummary = null, afterSummary = "FAILED: No backup passphrase configured",
56	                    reason = "backup_passphrase policy not set", sessionId = null,
57	                    outcome = AuditOutcome.FAILURE, timestamp = now, metadata = null
58	                ))
59	                return Result.failure()
60	            }
61	
62	            val dbFile = applicationContext.getDatabasePath("learnmart_encrypted.db")
63	            if (!dbFile.exists()) return Result.failure()
64	
65	            val backupDir = File(applicationContext.filesDir, "backups")
66	            backupDir.mkdirs()
67	            val backupFile = File(backupDir, "learnmart_backup_$archiveId.enc")
68	
69	            // PBKDF2 key derivation
70	            val salt = ByteArray(32).also { SecureRandom().nextBytes(it) }
71	            val iv = ByteArray(12).also { SecureRandom().nextBytes(it) }
72	            val spec = PBEKeySpec(passphrase.toCharArray(), salt, 120_000, 256)
73	            val secretKey = SecretKeySpec(
74	                SecretKeyFactory.getInstance("PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256").generateSecret(spec).encoded, "AES"
75	            )
76	
77	            val cipher = Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding")
78	            cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, secretKey, GCMParameterSpec(128, iv))
79	
80	            FileOutputStream(backupFile).use { fos ->
81	                fos.write(salt.size)
82	                fos.write(salt)
83	                fos.write(iv.size)
84	                fos.write(iv)
85	                CipherOutputStream(fos, cipher).use { cos ->
86	                    FileInputStream(dbFile).use { fis -> fis.copyTo(cos, 8192) }
87	                }
88	            }
89	
90	            // Checksum
91	            val digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256")
92	            FileInputStream(backupFile).use { fis ->
93	                val buf = ByteArray(8192); var n: Int
94	                while (fis.read(buf).also { n = it } != -1) digest.update(buf, 0, n)
95	            }
96	            val checksum = digest.digest().joinToString("") { "%02x".format(it) }
97	
98	            val archive = BackupArchive(
99	                id = archiveId, status = BackupStatus.SUCCEEDED,
100	                schemaVersion = 5, appVersion = "1.0.0",
101	                backupTimestamp = now, filePath = backupFile.absolutePath,
102	                fileSizeBytes = backupFile.length(), checksumManifest = checksum,
103	                encryptionMethod = "AES-256-GCM/PBKDF2",
104	                createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
105	            )
106	            operationsRepository.createBackupArchive(archive)
107	
108	            auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
109	                id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = "SYSTEM", actorUsername = "SYSTEM",
110	                actionType = AuditActionType.BACKUP_COMPLETED,
111	                targetEntityType = "BackupArchive", targetEntityId = archiveId,
112	                beforeSummary = null, afterSummary = "size=${backupFile.length()}, checksum=${checksum.take(16)}",
113	                reason = null, sessionId = null,
114	                outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS, timestamp = now, metadata = null
115	            ))
116	
117	            Result.success()
118	        } catch (e: Exception) {
119	            if (runAttemptCount < 4) Result.retry() else Result.failure()
120	        }
121	    }
122	}

69	// ViewModel

71	
72	data class ReconciliationUiState(
73	    val batchId: String = "",
74	    val isRunning: Boolean = false,
75	    val isCompleted: Boolean = false,
76	    val reconciliationRun: ReconciliationRun? = null,
77	    val discrepancyCases: List<DiscrepancyCase> = emptyList(),
78	    val isResolvingId: String? = null,
79	    val errorMessage: String? = null
80	)
81	
82	@HiltViewModel
83	class ReconciliationViewModel @Inject constructor(
84	    savedStateHandle: SavedStateHandle,
85	    private val reconciliationUseCase: ReconciliationUseCase
86	) : ViewModel() {
87	
88	    private val batchId: String = checkNotNull(savedStateHandle["batchId"])
89	
90	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(ReconciliationUiState(batchId = batchId))
91	    val uiState: StateFlow<ReconciliationUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
92	
93	    fun runReconciliation() {
94	        viewModelScope.launch {
95	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isRunning = true, errorMessage = null) }
96	            when (val result = reconciliationUseCase.runReconciliation(batchId)) {
97	                is AppResult.Success -> {
98	                    val discrepancies = reconciliationUseCase.getOpenDiscrepancies()
99	                    _uiState.update {
100	                        it.copy(
101	                            isRunning = false,
102	                            isCompleted = true,
103	                            reconciliationRun = result.data,
104	                            discrepancyCases = discrepancies
105	                        )
106	                    }
107	                }
108	                is AppResult.ValidationError -> {
109	                    _uiState.update {
110	                        it.copy(
111	                            isRunning = false,
112	                            errorMessage = result.globalErrors.firstOrNull()
113	                                ?: result.fieldErrors.values.firstOrNull()
114	                                ?: "Validation error"
115	                        )
116	                    }
117	                }
118	                is AppResult.PermissionError -> {
119	                    _uiState.update {
120	                        it.copy(isRunning = false, errorMessage = "Permission denied")
121	                    }
122	                }
123	                is AppResult.NotFoundError -> {
124	                    _uiState.update {
125	                        it.copy(isRunning = false, errorMessage = "Batch not found")
126	                    }
127	                }
128	                is AppResult.ConflictError -> {
129	                    _uiState.update {
130	                        it.copy(isRunning = false, errorMessage = result.message)
131	                    }
132	                }
133	                is AppResult.SystemError -> {
134	                    _uiState.update {
135	                        it.copy(isRunning = false, errorMessage = result.message)
136	                    }
137	                }
138	            }
139	        }
140	    }
141	
142	    fun resolveDiscrepancy(caseId: String, resolutionNote: String) {
143	        viewModelScope.launch {
144	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isResolvingId = caseId, errorMessage = null) }
145	            when (val result = reconciliationUseCase.resolveDiscrepancy(caseId, resolutionNote)) {
146	                is AppResult.Success -> {
147	                    val updatedCases = _uiState.value.discrepancyCases.map { case_ ->
148	                        if (case_.id == caseId) result.data else case_
149	                    }
150	                    _uiState.update {
151	                        it.copy(isResolvingId = null, discrepancyCases = updatedCases)
152	                    }
153	                }
154	                is AppResult.ValidationError -> {
155	                    _uiState.update {
156	                        it.copy(
157	                            isResolvingId = null,
158	                            errorMessage = result.globalErrors.firstOrNull()
159	                                ?: result.fieldErrors.values.firstOrNull()
160	                                ?: "Validation error"
161	                        )
162	                    }
163	                }
164	                is AppResult.PermissionError -> {
165	                    _uiState.update {
166	                        it.copy(isResolvingId = null, errorMessage = "Permission denied")
167	                    }
168	                }
169	                is AppResult.NotFoundError -> {
170	                    _uiState.update {
171	                        it.copy(isResolvingId = null, errorMessage = "Discrepancy case not found")
172	                    }
173	                }
174	                is AppResult.ConflictError -> {
175	                    _uiState.update {
176	                        it.copy(isResolvingId = null, errorMessage = result.message)
177	                    }
178	                }
179	                is AppResult.SystemError -> {
180	                    _uiState.update {
181	                        it.copy(isResolvingId = null, errorMessage = result.message)
182	                    }
183	                }
184	            }
185	        }
186	    }
187	
188	    fun clearError() {
189	        _uiState.update { it.copy(errorMessage = null) }
190	    }
191	}

500	@Composable
501	private fun ReconciliationDiscrepancyStatusChip(status: DiscrepancyCaseStatus) {
502	    val (label, containerColor, contentColor) = when (status) {
503	        DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN -> Triple(

68	
69	data class OperationsUiState(
70	    val importJobs: List<ImportJob> = emptyList(),
71	    val discrepancyCases: List<DiscrepancyCase> = emptyList(),
72	    val backupArchives: List<BackupArchive> = emptyList(),
73	    val exportJobs: List<ExportJob> = emptyList(),
74	    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
75	    val errorMessage: String? = null
76	)
77	
78	@HiltViewModel
79	class OperationsViewModel @Inject constructor(
80	    private val manageOperationsUseCase: ManageOperationsUseCase
81	) : ViewModel() {
82	
83	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(OperationsUiState())
84	    val uiState: StateFlow<OperationsUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
85	
86	    init {
87	        loadAllData()
88	    }
89	
90	    fun loadAllData() {
91	        viewModelScope.launch {
92	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = true, errorMessage = null) }
93	            try {
94	                val importsResult = manageOperationsUseCase.getImportJobs()
95	                val discrepanciesResult = manageOperationsUseCase.getDiscrepancyCases(limit = 100, offset = 0)
96	                val backupsResult = manageOperationsUseCase.getBackupArchives()
97	                val exportsResult = manageOperationsUseCase.getExportJobs(limit = 100, offset = 0)
98	
99	                val imports = when (importsResult) {
100	                    is AppResult.Success -> importsResult.data
101	                    is AppResult.PermissionError -> { _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, errorMessage = importsResult.code) }; return@launch }
102	                    else -> emptyList()
103	                }
104	                val discrepancies = when (discrepanciesResult) {
105	                    is AppResult.Success -> discrepanciesResult.data
106	                    is AppResult.PermissionError -> { _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, errorMessage = discrepanciesResult.code) }; return@launch }
107	                    else -> emptyList()
108	                }
109	                val backups = when (backupsResult) {
110	                    is AppResult.Success -> backupsResult.data
111	                    is AppResult.PermissionError -> { _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, errorMessage = backupsResult.code) }; return@launch }
112	                    else -> emptyList()
113	                }
114	                val exports = when (exportsResult) {
115	                    is AppResult.Success -> exportsResult.data
116	                    is AppResult.PermissionError -> { _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, errorMessage = exportsResult.code) }; return@launch }
117	                    else -> emptyList()
118	                }
119	
120	                _uiState.update {
121	                    it.copy(
122	                        importJobs = imports,
123	                        discrepancyCases = discrepancies,
124	                        backupArchives = backups,
125	                        exportJobs = exports,
126	                        isLoading = false
127	                    )
128	                }
129	            } catch (e: Exception) {
130	                _uiState.update {
131	                    it.copy(isLoading = false, errorMessage = e.message ?: "Failed to load operations data")
132	                }
133	            }
134	        }
135	    }
136	}

141	
142	private val operationsDateFormatter: DateTimeFormatter =
143	    DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("MMM dd, yyyy HH:mm")
144	        .withZone(ZoneId.systemDefault())
145	
146	private val tabTitles = listOf("Imports", "Discrepancies", "Backups", "Exports")
147	
148	@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
149	@Composable
150	fun OperationsScreen(
151	    onNavigateToImport: () -> Unit,
152	    onNavigateToReconciliation: (String) -> Unit,
153	    onNavigateToBackupDetail: (String) -> Unit,
154	    onNavigateBack: () -> Unit,
155	    viewModel: OperationsViewModel = hiltViewModel()
156	) {
157	    val state by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
158	    var selectedTabIndex by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
159	
160	    Scaffold(
161	        topBar = {
162	            TopAppBar(
163	                title = { Text("Operations") },
164	                navigationIcon = {
165	                    IconButton(onClick = onNavigateBack) {
166	                        Icon(
167	                            imageVector = Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack,
168	                            contentDescription = "Back"
169	                        )
170	                    }
171	                },
172	                colors = TopAppBarDefaults.topAppBarColors(
173	                    containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary,
174	                    titleContentColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary,
175	                    navigationIconContentColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary
176	                )
177	            )
178	        }
179	    ) { paddingValues ->
180	        Column(
181	            modifier = Modifier
182	                .fillMaxSize()
183	                .padding(paddingValues)
184	        ) {
185	            TabRow(selectedTabIndex = selectedTabIndex) {
186	                tabTitles.forEachIndexed { index, title ->
187	                    Tab(
188	                        selected = selectedTabIndex == index,
189	                        onClick = { selectedTabIndex = index },
190	                        text = { Text(title) }
191	                    )
192	                }
193	            }
194	
195	            if (state.isLoading) {
196	                Box(
197	                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
198	                    contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
199	                ) {
200	                    CircularProgressIndicator()
201	                }
202	            } else if (state.errorMessage != null) {
203	                Box(
204	                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
205	                    contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
206	                ) {
207	                    Text(
208	                        text = state.errorMessage ?: "Unknown error",
209	                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
210	                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error
211	                    )
212	                }
213	            } else {
214	                when (selectedTabIndex) {
215	                    0 -> ImportsTab(
216	                        importJobs = state.importJobs,
217	                        onNavigateToImport = onNavigateToImport,
218	                        onNavigateToReconciliation = onNavigateToReconciliation
219	                    )
220	                    1 -> DiscrepanciesTab(discrepancyCases = state.discrepancyCases)
221	                    2 -> BackupsTab(
222	                        backupArchives = state.backupArchives,
223	                        onNavigateToBackupDetail = onNavigateToBackupDetail
224	                    )
225	                    3 -> ExportsTab(exportJobs = state.exportJobs)
226	                }
227	            }
228	        }
229	    }
230	}

64	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	// ViewModel
66	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	
68	data class ImportUiState(
69	    val selectedFileName: String = "",
70	    val fileType: String = "csv",
71	    val fileContentBytes: ByteArray? = null,
72	    val signatureHex: String = "",
73	    val isSubmitting: Boolean = false,
74	    val isSubmitted: Boolean = false,
75	    val resultJob: ImportJob? = null,
76	    val warnings: List<String> = emptyList(),
77	    val errorMessage: String? = null
78	)
79	
80	@HiltViewModel
81	class ImportViewModel @Inject constructor(
82	    private val importSettlementUseCase: ImportSettlementUseCase
83	) : ViewModel() {
84	
85	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(ImportUiState())
86	    val uiState: StateFlow<ImportUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
87	
88	    fun onFileSelected(uri: Uri, context: Context) {
89	        val contentBytes = context.contentResolver.openInputStream(uri)?.readBytes()
90	        if (contentBytes == null) {
91	            _uiState.update { it.copy(errorMessage = "Could not read the selected file") }
92	            return
93	        }
94	
95	        val fileName = resolveFileName(uri, context)
96	        val fileType = when {
97	            fileName.endsWith(".csv", ignoreCase = true) -> "csv"
98	            fileName.endsWith(".json", ignoreCase = true) -> "json"
99	            else -> "csv"
100	        }
101	
102	        _uiState.update {
103	            it.copy(
104	                selectedFileName = fileName,
105	                fileType = fileType,
106	                fileContentBytes = contentBytes,
107	                errorMessage = null
108	            )
109	        }
110	    }
111	
112	    fun updateSignatureHex(value: String) {
113	        _uiState.update { it.copy(signatureHex = value) }
114	    }
115	
116	    fun clearError() {
117	        _uiState.update { it.copy(errorMessage = null) }
118	    }
119	
120	    fun startImport() {
121	        val current = _uiState.value
122	        if (current.selectedFileName.isBlank() || current.fileContentBytes == null) {
123	            _uiState.update { it.copy(errorMessage = "Please select a file first") }
124	            return
125	        }
126	
127	        viewModelScope.launch {
128	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isSubmitting = true, errorMessage = null) }
129	
130	            val contentString = current.fileContentBytes.decodeToString()
131	            val rawRows = parseContent(contentString, current.fileType)
132	            if (rawRows == null) {
133	                _uiState.update {
134	                    it.copy(isSubmitting = false, errorMessage = "Could not parse file content. Ensure the file is valid ${current.fileType.uppercase()}.")
135	                }
136	                return@launch
137	            }
138	
139	            val fileSizeBytes = current.fileContentBytes.size.toLong()
140	            when (val result = importSettlementUseCase.importFile(
141	                fileName = current.selectedFileName,
142	                fileType = current.fileType,
143	                fileSizeBytes = fileSizeBytes,
144	                rawRows = rawRows,
145	                fileContent = current.fileContentBytes,
146	                signatureHex = current.signatureHex.ifBlank { null }
147	            )) {
148	                is AppResult.Success -> {
149	                    _uiState.update {
150	                        it.copy(
151	                            isSubmitting = false,
152	                            isSubmitted = true,
153	                            resultJob = result.data,
154	                            warnings = result.warnings
155	                        )
156	                    }
157	                }
158	                is AppResult.ValidationError -> {
159	                    _uiState.update {
160	                        it.copy(
161	                            isSubmitting = false,
162	                            errorMessage = result.globalErrors.firstOrNull()
163	                                ?: result.fieldErrors.values.firstOrNull()
164	                                ?: "Validation error"
165	                        )
166	                    }
167	                }
168	                is AppResult.PermissionError -> {
169	                    _uiState.update {
170	                        it.copy(isSubmitting = false, errorMessage = "Permission denied")
171	                    }
172	                }
173	                is AppResult.ConflictError -> {
174	                    _uiState.update {
175	                        it.copy(isSubmitting = false, errorMessage = result.message)
176	                    }
177	                }
178	                is AppResult.NotFoundError -> {
179	                    _uiState.update {
180	                        it.copy(isSubmitting = false, errorMessage = "Resource not found")
181	                    }
182	                }
183	                is AppResult.SystemError -> {
184	                    _uiState.update {
185	                        it.copy(isSubmitting = false, errorMessage = result.message)
186	                    }
187	                }
188	            }
189	        }
190	    }
191	
192	    fun resetForm() {
193	        _uiState.update { ImportUiState() }
194	    }
195	
196	    private fun resolveFileName(uri: Uri, context: Context): String {
197	        var name = "unknown_file"
198	        context.contentResolver.query(uri, null, null, null, null)?.use { cursor ->
199	            val nameIndex = cursor.getColumnIndex(OpenableColumns.DISPLAY_NAME)
200	            if (nameIndex >= 0 && cursor.moveToFirst()) {
201	                name = cursor.getString(nameIndex) ?: name
202	            }
203	        }
204	        return name
205	    }
206	
207	    private fun parseContent(content: String, fileType: String): List<Map<String, String>>? {
208	        return when (fileType) {
209	            "csv" -> parseCsv(content)
210	            "json" -> parseJson(content)
211	            else -> parseCsv(content)
212	        }
213	    }
214	
215	    private fun parseCsv(csv: String): List<Map<String, String>>? {
216	        return try {
217	            val lines = csv.lines().filter { it.isNotBlank() }
218	            if (lines.size < 2) return null
219	            val headers = lines.first().split(",").map { it.trim().removeSurrounding("\"") }
220	            val rows = mutableListOf<Map<String, String>>()
221	            for (line in lines.drop(1)) {
222	                val values = line.split(",").map { it.trim().removeSurrounding("\"") }
223	                val map = mutableMapOf<String, String>()
224	                headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
225	                    if (index < values.size) {
226	                        map[header] = values[index]
227	                    }
228	                }
229	                if (map.isNotEmpty()) rows.add(map)
230	            }
231	            if (rows.isEmpty()) null else rows
232	        } catch (_: Exception) {
233	            null
234	        }
235	    }
236	
237	    private fun parseJson(json: String): List<Map<String, String>>? {
238	        return try {
239	            val trimmed = json.trim()
240	            if (!trimmed.startsWith("[")) return null
241	            val result = mutableListOf<Map<String, String>>()
242	            val objectPattern = Regex("\\{([^}]*)\\}")
243	            val kvPattern = Regex("\"([^\"]+)\"\\s*:\\s*\"([^\"]*)\"")
244	            for (match in objectPattern.findAll(trimmed)) {
245	                val map = mutableMapOf<String, String>()
246	                for (kv in kvPattern.findAll(match.groupValues[1])) {
247	                    map[kv.groupValues[1]] = kv.groupValues[2]
248	                }
249	                if (map.isNotEmpty()) {
250	                    result.add(map)
251	                }
252	            }
253	            if (result.isEmpty()) null else result
254	        } catch (_: Exception) {
255	            null
256	        }
257	    }
258	}

269	) {

287	    Scaffold(

294	                    }
295	                },
296	                colors = TopAppBarDefaults.topAppBarColors(
297	                    containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary,
298	                    titleContentColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary,
299	                    navigationIconContentColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onPrimary
300	                )
301	            )
302	        },

304	    ) { padding ->

307	                .fillMaxSize()
308	                .padding(padding)
309	                .verticalScroll(rememberScrollState())
310	                .padding(16.dp)
311	        ) {
312	            if (state.isSubmitted && state.resultJob != null) {
313	                ImportResultContent(
314	                    job = state.resultJob!!,
315	                    warnings = state.warnings,
316	                    onReset = viewModel::resetForm,
317	                    onNavigateBack = onNavigateBack
318	                )
319	            } else {
320	                ImportFormContent(
321	                    state = state,
322	                    onSelectFile = {
323	                        filePickerLauncher.launch(arrayOf("text/csv", "application/json", "text/*"))
324	                    },
325	                    onSignatureHexChanged = viewModel::updateSignatureHex,
326	                    onSubmit = viewModel::startImport
327	                )
328	            }
329	        }
330	    }
331	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.ui.screens.dashboard
2	
3	import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
4	import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.AuditEvent
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.Permission
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.RoleType
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.UserRepository
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.LogoutUseCase
11	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.audit.ViewAuditLogUseCase
12	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
13	import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
14	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
15	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
16	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
17	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
18	import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
19	import javax.inject.Inject
20	
21	data class DashboardUiState(
22	    val displayName: String = "",
23	    val roles: List<RoleType> = emptyList(),
24	    val recentAuditEvents: List<AuditEvent> = emptyList(),
25	    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
26	    val isLoggedOut: Boolean = false
27	)
28	
29	@HiltViewModel
30	class DashboardViewModel @Inject constructor(
31	    private val checkPermissionUseCase: CheckPermissionUseCase,
32	    private val logoutUseCase: LogoutUseCase,
33	    private val viewAuditLogUseCase: ViewAuditLogUseCase,
34	    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
35	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
36	) : ViewModel() {
37	
38	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(DashboardUiState())
39	    val uiState: StateFlow<DashboardUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
40	
41	    init {
42	        loadDashboardData()
43	    }
44	
45	    private fun loadDashboardData() {
46	        viewModelScope.launch {
47	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = true) }
48	
49	            val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId()
50	            if (userId == null) {
51	                _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, isLoggedOut = true) }
52	                return@launch
53	            }
54	
55	            val user = userRepository.getUserById(userId)
56	            val displayName = user?.displayName ?: ""
57	
58	            val roles = checkPermissionUseCase.getCurrentUserRoles()
59	
60	            _uiState.update {
61	                it.copy(
62	                    displayName = displayName,
63	                    roles = roles
64	                )
65	            }
66	
67	            collectRecentAuditEvents()
68	        }
69	    }
70	
71	    private fun collectRecentAuditEvents() {
72	        viewModelScope.launch {
73	            viewAuditLogUseCase.getRecentEvents(limit = 10)
74	                .collect { events ->
75	                    _uiState.update {
76	                        it.copy(
77	                            recentAuditEvents = events,
78	                            isLoading = false
79	                        )
80	                    }
81	                }
82	        }
83	    }
84	
85	    fun logout() {
86	        viewModelScope.launch {
87	            logoutUseCase()
88	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoggedOut = true) }
89	        }
90	    }
91	
92	    suspend fun hasPermission(permission: Permission): Boolean {
93	        return checkPermissionUseCase.hasPermission(permission)
94	    }
95	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.operations
2	
3	import androidx.room.withTransaction
4	import com.learnmart.app.data.local.LearnMartRoomDatabase
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OperationsRepository
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
10	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
11	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
12	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
13	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
14	import javax.inject.Inject
15	
16	class ReconciliationUseCase @Inject constructor(
17	    private val operationsRepository: OperationsRepository,
18	    private val paymentRepository: PaymentRepository,
19	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
20	    private val checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase,
21	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager,
22	    private val database: LearnMartRoomDatabase
23	) {
24	    suspend fun runReconciliation(batchId: String): AppResult<ReconciliationRun> {
25	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.PAYMENT_RECONCILE)) {
26	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Requires payment.reconcile")
27	        }
28	
29	        val batch = operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById(batchId)
30	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("BATCH_NOT_FOUND")
31	
32	        val rows = operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch(batchId)
33	        val validRows = rows.filter { it.isValid && !it.isDuplicate }
34	
35	        if (validRows.isEmpty()) {
36	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("No valid rows to reconcile"))
37	        }
38	
39	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
40	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM"
41	        val runId = IdGenerator.newId()
42	
43	        val matches = mutableListOf<ReconciliationMatch>()
44	        val discrepancies = mutableListOf<DiscrepancyCase>()
45	        var matchedCount = 0
46	        var unmatchedCount = 0
47	        val duplicateCount = rows.count { it.isDuplicate }
48	
49	        for (row in validRows) {
50	            val paymentRef = row.paymentReference
51	            if (paymentRef == null) {
52	                unmatchedCount++
53	                discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
54	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
55	                    settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
56	                    discrepancyType = "NO_REFERENCE", description = "Settlement row has no payment reference",
57	                    notes = "Row external_id: ${row.externalRowId}", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
58	                    resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
59	                    createdBy = userId, createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
60	                ))
61	                continue
62	            }
63	
64	            // Try to match against payment records by external reference
65	            val payments = paymentRepository.getPaymentsForOrder(paymentRef)
66	            // Also try matching by payment ID directly
67	            val directMatch = paymentRepository.getPaymentById(paymentRef)
68	
69	            val matchedPayment = directMatch ?: payments.firstOrNull()
70	
71	            if (matchedPayment != null) {
72	                // Amount check
73	                if (matchedPayment.amount.compareTo(row.amount) != 0) {
74	                    discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
75	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
76	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
77	                        discrepancyType = "AMOUNT_MISMATCH",
78	                        description = "Settlement amount ${row.amount} != payment amount ${matchedPayment.amount}",
79	                        notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
80	                        resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
81	                        createdBy = userId, createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
82	                    ))
83	                }
84	
85	                matches.add(ReconciliationMatch(
86	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
87	                    settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
88	                    matchType = "REFERENCE", matchedAt = now
89	                ))
90	                matchedCount++
91	            } else {
92	                unmatchedCount++
93	                discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
94	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
95	                    settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
96	                    discrepancyType = "UNMATCHED", description = "No matching payment found for reference: $paymentRef",
97	                    notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
98	                    resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
99	                    createdBy = userId, createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
100	                ))
101	            }
102	        }
103	
104	        // Atomic: create run + matches + discrepancies + update import job
105	        // Wrapped in a Room transaction so all writes succeed or roll back together
106	        val run = ReconciliationRun(
107	            id = runId, batchId = batchId,
108	            matchedCount = matchedCount, unmatchedCount = unmatchedCount,
109	            duplicateCount = duplicateCount, discrepancyCount = discrepancies.size,
110	            runBy = userId, runAt = now, status = "COMPLETED"
111	        )
112	        database.withTransaction {
113	            operationsRepository.createReconciliationRun(run)
114	            if (matches.isNotEmpty()) operationsRepository.createReconciliationMatches(matches)
115	            discrepancies.forEach { operationsRepository.createDiscrepancyCase(it) }
116	
117	            // Update import job
118	            val importJob = operationsRepository.getImportJobById(batch.importJobId)
119	            if (importJob != null) {
120	                operationsRepository.updateImportJob(importJob.copy(
121	                    status = ImportJobStatus.APPLIED, updatedAt = now
122	                ))
123	            }
124	        }
125	
126	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
127	            id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = userId, actorUsername = null,
128	            actionType = AuditActionType.RECONCILIATION_RUN,
129	            targetEntityType = "ReconciliationRun", targetEntityId = runId,
130	            beforeSummary = null,
131	            afterSummary = "matched=$matchedCount, unmatched=$unmatchedCount, discrepancies=${discrepancies.size}",
132	            reason = null, sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
133	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS, timestamp = now, metadata = null
134	        ))
135	
136	        return AppResult.Success(run)
137	    }
138	
139	    suspend fun resolveDiscrepancy(caseId: String, resolutionNote: String): AppResult<DiscrepancyCase> {
140	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.PAYMENT_RECONCILE)) {
141	            return AppResult.PermissionError()
142	        }
143	        if (resolutionNote.isBlank()) {
144	            return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = mapOf("resolutionNote" to "Resolution note is required"))
145	        }
146	
147	        val case_ = operationsRepository.getDiscrepancyCaseById(caseId)
148	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("DISCREPANCY_NOT_FOUND")
149	
150	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
151	        val resolved = case_.copy(
152	            status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.RESOLVED,
153	            resolvedBy = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(), resolvedAt = now,
154	            resolutionNote = resolutionNote, updatedAt = now
155	        )
156	        operationsRepository.updateDiscrepancyCase(resolved)
157	
158	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
159	            id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(), actorUsername = null,
160	            actionType = AuditActionType.DISCREPANCY_RESOLVED,
161	            targetEntityType = "DiscrepancyCase", targetEntityId = caseId,
162	            beforeSummary = "status=${case_.status}", afterSummary = "status=RESOLVED",
163	            reason = resolutionNote, sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
164	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS, timestamp = now, metadata = null
165	        ))
166	
167	        return AppResult.Success(resolved)
168	    }
169	
170	    suspend fun getAllDiscrepancies(limit: Int, offset: Int): List<DiscrepancyCase> =
171	        operationsRepository.getAllDiscrepancyCases(limit, offset)
172	
173	    suspend fun getOpenDiscrepancies(): List<DiscrepancyCase> =
174	        operationsRepository.getDiscrepancyCasesByStatus(DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN)
175	}
